title: NPI and taxonomy correctness
headline: 95.72% of 10.9M NDH NPIs clear NPPES (0.79% ghost, 3.49% deactivated). Practitioner full-name match: 94.9%; Organization name match: 56.3%. NDH carries NUCC on Practitioner.qualification (99.83% valid) AND Medicare Specialty codes on PractitionerRole.specialty (99.98% valid against the CMS-published crosswalk). Internal cross-system consistency (Role CMS → crosswalk → Qualification NUCC): 85.8% of 3.3M Practitioner↔Role pairs agree. External NUCC agreement NDH↔NPPES: 92.0%.
notes: Corrections from earlier v0.1 of this finding: CMS DOES publish a Medicare Provider and Supplier Taxonomy Crosswalk (data.cms.gov/provider-characteristics/medicare-provider-supplier-enrollment/medicare-provider-and-supplier-taxonomy-crosswalk; 2025-10 release, 565 rows, 1-to-many). The prior "no cross-walk" framing was incorrect — consumers CAN and DO (Blue Button) bridge Medicare Specialty codes and NUCC via this table. NDH PractitionerRole._specialty_code carries values like '14-50' — the leading 'NN-' prefix does not appear in the crosswalk; stripping it recovers the canonical 2-character Medicare Specialty code used by the crosswalk's MEDICARE SPECIALTY CODE column. H12 methodology: NUCC codes on Practitioner.qualification validated against NUCC v17.0 (7,112,042 of 7,124,017); Medicare codes on PractitionerRole.specialty validated against the crosswalk's MEDICARE SPECIALTY CODE column (3,344,800 of 3,345,518). H13 methodology: internal — 3,337,053 Practitioner↔PractitionerRole pairs; a pair is crosswalk-consistent if (role.Medicare code, qualification.NUCC code) is a row in the crosswalk. External — 7,124,017 NDH NUCC qualifications compared against NPPES primary taxonomy; exact-code match. Known caveat: NPPES vintage 2026-02-09 vs NDH 2026-04-09 — 8-week gap means taxonomy changes in that window show as disagreement. v2 upgrade candidates: Jaro-Winkler name agreement (H11); NPPES secondary-taxonomy match as additional H13 signal; confusion matrix per specialty; pinned NUCC quarterly release.
